summary: The run produced reusable source-grounded event and negative-audit components, but did not solve the full generic memory-system goal.

rounds.failure: loose keyword matching did not answer the target method question
related method allegation was conflated with the requested method
generic procedural/conclusion terms bound to the wrong source issue
self-evaluation falsely passed unresolved direct-conclusion absence
negative audit was no longer broad enough

rounds.useful: best evidence checkpoint: factual method span plus broad negative search for the second method
rounds.failure: direct method conclusion still absent; hard-coded aspect rules remain
more candidates did not add grounded evidence; self-evaluation regressed

rounds.useful: best strict gate snapshot: unresolved direct conclusion correctly fails
rounds.failure: core findings still identical to the repeated frontier
self-evaluation overclaim returned; direct conclusion and genericity remained unresolved

grade.overall: D+ to C- as a full memory system; useful as a source-grounded audit component

takeaway: Do not compress first and do not trust a single keyword filter. Preserve raw source spans, use role-bound event/conclusion checks, and keep audited absence separate from positive answer grounding.
